 {"id":"bd-2dbf","title":"HPACK: Huffman invalid padding silently accepted (FIXED)","description":"# Bug Fix: Huffman Invalid Padding (COMPLETED)

## Status: FIXED in session 2026-01-31

## Location
`src/http/h2/hpack.rs` - huffman_decode function

## Vulnerability
The Huffman decoder silently accepted invalid padding (not all 1s) at the end
of encoded data. Per RFC 7541, padding must be the most-significant bits of EOS.
Invalid padding could indicate encoding errors or malicious data.

## Original Code
```rust
// End of decoding - didn't check padding validity
Ok(String::from_utf8(output).map_err(|_| H2Error::compression("invalid UTF-8"))?)
```

## Fix Applied
Validate padding bits are all 1s:

```rust
// After decoding, check remaining bits are valid padding
if bit_offset > 0 {
    // Remaining bits should be all 1s (padding is MSBs of EOS symbol)
    let padding_mask = (1u8 << (8 - bit_offset)) - 1;
    let padding_bits = current_byte & padding_mask;
    if padding_bits != padding_mask {
        return Err(H2Error::compression("invalid Huffman padding"));
    }
}
```

## Testing Required
- test_huffman_decode_invalid_padding
- test_huffman_decode_valid_padding

 {"id":"bd-2s95","title":"Message queue E2E test suite: NATS/Redis/Kafka integration tests","description":"# Message Queue E2E Test Suite

## Overview
Comprehensive end-to-end test suite for all message queue clients (NATS, Redis, Kafka)
with cancel-correctness verification and structured logging.

## Test Directory Structure
```
tests/e2e/mq/
├── mod.rs                       # Test module root
├── common/
│   ├── mod.rs                   # Shared utilities
│   ├── docker.rs                # Docker service management
│   └── assertions.rs            # Message queue assertions
├── nats/
│   ├── pubsub.rs                # Pub/sub tests
│   ├── request_reply.rs         # Request/reply pattern
│   ├── jetstream.rs             # JetStream durability
│   └── cancel.rs                # Cancellation tests
├── redis/
│   ├── commands.rs              # Basic command tests
│   ├── pubsub.rs                # Pub/sub tests
│   ├── streams.rs               # Streams consumer groups
│   └── cancel.rs                # Cancellation tests
├── kafka/
│   ├── producer.rs              # Producer tests
│   ├── consumer.rs              # Consumer group tests
│   ├── transactions.rs          # Transactional tests
│   └── cancel.rs                # Cancellation tests
└── lab/
    ├── deterministic.rs         # Lab runtime tests
    └── schedule_exploration.rs  # DPOR tests
```

## Docker Compose Setup
```yaml
version: '3.8'
services:
  nats:
    image: nats:latest
    ports:
      - "4222:4222"
    command: ["-js"]  # Enable JetStream
    
  redis:
    image: redis:7
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
    
  kafka:
    image: confluentinc/cp-kafka:latest
    ports:
      - "9092:9092"
    environment:
      KAFKA_BROKER_ID: 1
      KAFKA_LISTENERS: PLAINTEXT://0.0.0.0:9092
      KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS: PLAINTEXT://localhost:9092
      KAFKA_OFFSETS_TOPIC_REPLICATION_FACTOR: 1
      
  zookeeper:
    image: confluentinc/cp-zookeeper:latest
    environment:
      ZOOKEEPER_CLIENT_PORT: 2181
```

## Test Categories

### 1. Basic Functionality
- NATS: connect, publish, subscribe, wildcard
- Redis: GET/SET, HGET/HSET, EXPIRE
- Kafka: produce, consume, seek

### 2. Durability Tests
- NATS JetStream: persist, replay
- Redis Streams: consumer groups, ack
- Kafka: offset commit, rebalance

### 3. Cancel-Correctness Tests
- Subscription cancelled mid-receive
- Producer cancelled mid-send
- Consumer cancelled with uncommitted messages
- Region close with active connections

### 4. Backpressure Tests
- Slow consumer with fast producer
- Bounded queues fill up
- Credit window exhaustion

### 5. Lab Runtime Tests
- Deterministic message ordering
- Simulated network delays
- Partition simulation

## Logging Requirements

### Log Fields
```rust
tracing::info\!(
    test_name = %test_name,
    mq_type = %"nats",
    operation = %"publish",
    subject = %subject,
    message_count = count,
    latency_ms = latency.as_millis(),
    "Message queue operation"
);
```

### Metrics Collection
- Publish latency histogram
- Consume latency histogram
- Message throughput (msg/sec)
- Backpressure events
- Reconnection count

## Test Scripts

### Start Services
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
cd tests/e2e/mq
docker-compose up -d

# Wait for services
echo "Waiting for NATS..."
until nc -z localhost 4222; do sleep 1; done

echo "Waiting for Redis..."
until nc -z localhost 6379; do sleep 1; done

echo "Waiting for Kafka..."
until nc -z localhost 9092; do sleep 1; done

echo "All services ready"
```

### Run Tests
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

export RUST_LOG="asupersync=debug,test=info"
export NATS_URL="nats://localhost:4222"
export REDIS_URL="redis://localhost:6379"
export KAFKA_BROKERS="localhost:9092"

echo "=== Message Queue E2E Tests ==="

# Run tests by MQ type
for mq in nats redis kafka; do
    echo "--- Testing $mq ---"
    cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_mq $mq:: -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture 2>&1 | tee $mq_tests.log
done

echo "=== Summary ==="
grep -E '(PASS|FAIL|test result)' *_tests.log
```

### Cleanup
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
cd tests/e2e/mq
docker-compose down -v
```

## Dependencies
- Requires: All MQ implementations (bd-3f53, bd-3kc4, bd-9vfn, bd-1z91, bd-3p7x, bd-1zr7)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All basic functionality tests pass
- [ ] Durability tests verify persistence
- [ ] Cancel-correctness verified
- [ ] Backpressure handling works
- [ ] Lab runtime tests deterministic
- [ ] Docker compose setup works
- [ ] Test scripts documented
- [ ] Structured logging with metrics
- [ ] CI integration (Docker services)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:27:34.223221334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:50:33.673101041Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:50:33.673020852Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","ecosystem-parity","messaging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s95","depends_on_id":"bd-1z91","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2s95","depends_on_id":"bd-1zr7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2s95","depends_on_id":"bd-2aiq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2s95","depends_on_id":"bd-3f53","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2s95","depends_on_id":"bd-3kc4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2s95","depends_on_id":"bd-3p7x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2s95","depends_on_id":"bd-9vfn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2sad","title":"HTTP/2 PRIORITY: validate stream cannot depend on itself","description":"# Bug: Stream Self-Dependency Not Validated in PRIORITY Frame

## Location
`src/http/h2/frame.rs:445-469`

## Issue Description

The PRIORITY frame parser extracts dependency information but never validates 
that a stream cannot depend on itself:

```rust
let exclusive = payload[0] & 0x80 != 0;
let dependency = ((u32::from(payload[0]) & 0x7f) << 24)
    | (u32::from(payload[1]) << 16)
    | (u32::from(payload[2]) << 8)
    | u32::from(payload[3]);
let weight = payload[4].saturating_add(1);
```

## RFC Requirement

RFC 7540 Section 5.3.1 states:
> A stream cannot depend on itself. An endpoint MUST treat this as a 
> stream error (Section 5.4.2) of type PROTOCOL_ERROR.

## Impact

- **Severity**: LOW (protocol violation, not security)
- **Exploitability**: N/A (no direct exploit, just non-compliance)
- **Impact**: Potential dependency tree corruption, conformance failure

## Proposed Fix

Add validation in frame parsing:

```rust
// In PriorityFrame::parse()
let dependency = ...;

if dependency == header.stream_id {
    return Err(H2Error::stream_error(
        header.stream_id,
        ErrorCode::ProtocolError,
        "stream cannot depend on itself",
    ));
}
```

---

## Comprehensive Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (tests/h2/priority_frame_tests.rs)

#### Self-Dependency Detection Tests
1. **test_priority_self_dependency_stream_1**: Stream 1 depends on 1 = stream error
2. **test_priority_self_dependency_stream_3**: Stream 3 depends on 3 = stream error
3. **test_priority_self_dependency_max_stream**: Stream 2^31-1 depends on self = error
4. **test_priority_self_dependency_error_code**: Error must be PROTOCOL_ERROR
5. **test_priority_self_dependency_stream_error**: Must be stream error, not connection error

#### Valid Dependency Tests
6. **test_priority_valid_dependency**: Stream 3 depends on 1 = success
7. **test_priority_root_dependency**: Stream depends on 0 (root) = success
8. **test_priority_reverse_dependency**: Higher stream depends on lower = success
9. **test_priority_forward_dependency**: Lower stream depends on higher = success

#### Exclusive Flag Tests
10. **test_priority_exclusive_with_self_dependency**: Exclusive + self-dep = error
11. **test_priority_exclusive_valid**: Exclusive on valid dependency = success
12. **test_priority_exclusive_root**: Exclusive dependency on root = success

#### Weight Tests
13. **test_priority_weight_min**: Weight 0 in payload = weight 1
14. **test_priority_weight_max**: Weight 255 in payload = weight 256
15. **test_priority_weight_mid**: Weight 127 in payload = weight 128

#### Frame Structure Tests
16. **test_priority_wrong_length**: Payload != 5 bytes = error
17. **test_priority_stream_0**: PRIORITY on stream 0 = connection error
18. **test_priority_headers_priority**: HEADERS with PRIORITY flag includes priority fields

### Integration Tests (tests/h2/priority_integration.rs)

```rust
#[test]
fn integration_priority_self_dep_rejected_by_server() {
    let (mut client, mut server) = create_connected_pair();
    
    // Client sends PRIORITY with self-dependency
    client.send_priority(/*stream=*/ 1, /*depends_on=*/ 1, /*weight=*/ 16, /*exclusive=*/ false);
    
    // Server should send RST_STREAM with PROTOCOL_ERROR
    let frame = server.receive_frame();
    if let Some(Frame::RstStream(rst)) = frame {
        assert_eq!(rst.stream_id, 1);
        assert_eq!(rst.error_code, ErrorCode::ProtocolError);
    } else {
        panic!("expected RST_STREAM, got {:?}", frame);
    }
}

#[test]
fn integration_priority_self_dep_via_headers() {
    let (mut client, mut server) = create_connected_pair();
    
    // HEADERS frame with PRIORITY flag where stream depends on itself
    client.send_headers_with_priority(
        /*stream=*/ 3,
        /*depends_on=*/ 3, // self-dependency via HEADERS
        /*weight=*/ 16,
        /*exclusive=*/ false,
        &[(":method", "GET"), (":path", "/")],
    );
    
    // Should be rejected
    let frame = server.receive_frame();
    assert!(matches!(frame, Some(Frame::RstStream(_)) | Some(Frame::GoAway(_))));
}
```

### Conformance Tests (tests/h2/priority_conformance.rs)

```rust
/// h2spec test: 5.3.1 - Stream Dependencies - A stream cannot depend on itself
#[test]
fn h2spec_5_3_1_self_dependency() {
    let mut conn = Connection::new_server();
    
    // Receive valid HEADERS to open stream 1
    conn.process_headers(1, &[(":method", "GET")], END_HEADERS).unwrap();
    
    // Receive PRIORITY with self-dependency
    let result = conn.process_priority(/*stream=*/ 1, /*dep=*/ 1, 16, false);
    
    // Must return stream error with PROTOCOL_ERROR
    match result {
        Err(H2Error::StreamError { stream_id, error_code, .. }) => {
            assert_eq!(stream_id, 1);
            assert_eq!(error_code, ErrorCode::ProtocolError);
        }
        other => panic!("expected StreamError, got {:?}", other),
    }
}
```

### Dependency Tree Tests (tests/h2/priority_tree_tests.rs)

```rust
#[test]
fn test_dependency_tree_no_cycles() {
    let mut tree = DependencyTree::new();
    
    // Build tree: 1 -> root, 3 -> 1, 5 -> 3
    tree.set_dependency(1, 0, 16, false);
    tree.set_dependency(3, 1, 16, false);
    tree.set_dependency(5, 3, 16, false);
    
    // Verify no cycles
    assert!(!tree.has_cycle());
    
    // Self-dependency should be rejected before tree mutation
    // (validation at frame level, not tree level)
}

#[test]
fn test_dependency_tree_exclusive_repararent() {
    let mut tree = DependencyTree::new();
    
    // 1, 3, 5 all depend on root
    tree.set_dependency(1, 0, 16, false);
    tree.set_dependency(3, 0, 16, false);
    tree.set_dependency(5, 0, 16, false);
    
    // Stream 7 exclusively depends on root
    // -> 1, 3, 5 should now depend on 7
    tree.set_dependency(7, 0, 16, true);
    
    assert_eq!(tree.parent(1), Some(7));
    assert_eq!(tree.parent(3), Some(7));
    assert_eq!(tree.parent(5), Some(7));
    assert_eq!(tree.parent(7), Some(0));
}
```

### Stress Tests (tests/h2/priority_stress.rs)

```rust
#[test]
fn stress_priority_many_streams() {
    let mut conn = Connection::new_server();
    
    // Open 1000 streams with various dependencies
    for i in 1..=1000u32 {
        let stream = i * 2 - 1; // Odd stream IDs (client-initiated)
        let depends_on = if i == 1 { 0 } else { (i - 1) * 2 - 1 };
        
        conn.process_headers(stream, &[(":method", "GET")], END_HEADERS).unwrap();
        conn.process_priority(stream, depends_on, (i % 256) as u8, i % 2 == 0).unwrap();
    }
    
    // Verify tree integrity
    assert!(!conn.dependency_tree().has_cycle());
    assert_eq!(conn.dependency_tree().depth(), 1000);
}

#[test]
fn stress_priority_all_self_dep_rejected() {
    let mut conn = Connection::new_server();
    
    for stream in (1..=1000u32).map(|i| i * 2 - 1) {
        conn.process_headers(stream, &[(":method", "GET")], END_HEADERS).unwrap();
        
        // Each self-dependency should fail
        let result = conn.process_priority(stream, stream, 16, false);
        assert!(result.is_err());
    }
}
```

### Fuzz Testing (fuzz/priority_frame.rs)

```rust
#![no_main]
use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;

fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
    if data.len() != 5 { return; } // PRIORITY payload is exactly 5 bytes
    
    let mut conn = Connection::new_server();
    
    // Open a stream first
    let _ = conn.process_headers(1, &[(":method", "GET")], END_HEADERS);
    
    // Feed fuzzed PRIORITY frame
    let _ = conn.process_priority_bytes(1, data);
    
    // Should not panic, invariants maintained
    assert!(conn.is_state_consistent());
});
```

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-2t4n","title":"Scheduler backoff: re-check queues after parking","description":"# Critical Bug: Backoff-to-Park Lost Wakeup Race

## Location
`src/runtime/scheduler/worker.rs:150-168`
`src/runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs:228-264`

## Vulnerability Description

The backoff loop parks without re-checking the condition after waking:

```rust
loop {
    if !self.local.is_empty() || !self.global.is_empty() {
        break;
    }
    
    if backoff < SPIN_LIMIT {
        std::hint::spin_loop();
        backoff += 1;
    } else if backoff < SPIN_LIMIT + YIELD_LIMIT {
        std::thread::yield_now();
        backoff += 1;
    } else {
        self.parker.park();
        break;  // <-- BUG: No re-check after waking!
    }
}
```

## Race Condition

```
Worker:                        Task Injector:
───────                        ──────────────
is_empty() == true
backoff...
backoff...
                               inject(task)
                               unpark(worker)
parker.park()                  
park returns (woken)
break (exits loop)
continues to next phase
... never checks queue again
```

The worker was woken because work arrived, but it breaks out of the 
backoff loop without processing that work.

## Additional Issue in three_lane.rs

The three_lane version only checks global queue in backoff, not local:

```rust
loop {
    if !self.global.is_empty() {  // Only checks global!
        break;
    }
    // ...
}
```

This can cause unnecessary spinning when local queue has work.

## Impact

- **Severity**: CRITICAL (tasks left unprocessed)
- **Exploitability**: Timing-dependent, more likely under load
- **Impact**: Increased latency, potential task starvation

## Proposed Fix

After park() returns, re-check the condition instead of breaking:

```rust
loop {
    // Check all queues
    if !self.local.is_empty() || !self.global.is_empty() {
        break;
    }
    
    if backoff < SPIN_LIMIT {
        std::hint::spin_loop();
        backoff += 1;
    } else if backoff < SPIN_LIMIT + YIELD_LIMIT {
        std::thread::yield_now();
        backoff += 1;
    } else {
        self.parker.park();
        // Continue loop to re-check condition (no break!)
        backoff = 0;  // Reset backoff after waking
    }
}
```

## Why Reset Backoff?

After being woken, the worker should:
1. Re-check queues (handled by loop continuation)
2. If work found, process it
3. If no work (spurious wakeup), spin briefly before parking again

---

## Comprehensive Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (tests/scheduler/backoff_tests.rs)

#### Core Logic Tests
1. **test_backoff_finds_work_in_local**: Work in local queue breaks loop immediately
2. **test_backoff_finds_work_in_global**: Work in global queue breaks loop
3. **test_backoff_checks_both_queues**: Both queues checked each iteration
4. **test_backoff_spin_count**: SPIN_LIMIT iterations of spin_loop
5. **test_backoff_yield_count**: YIELD_LIMIT iterations of yield_now
6. **test_backoff_parks_after_limits**: Parks after spin+yield limits reached

#### Race Condition Tests
7. **test_backoff_wakeup_during_spin**: Work arrives during spin phase
8. **test_backoff_wakeup_during_yield**: Work arrives during yield phase
9. **test_backoff_wakeup_during_park**: Work arrives during park - must find it
10. **test_backoff_spurious_wakeup**: Spurious wake with no work - re-spin
11. **test_backoff_multiple_wakeups**: Multiple unparks before park returns

#### Edge Cases
12. **test_backoff_local_only**: Local has work, global empty
13. **test_backoff_global_only**: Global has work, local empty
14. **test_backoff_both_have_work**: Both queues have work
15. **test_backoff_work_drained_during_backoff**: Work taken by other worker

### Stress Tests (tests/scheduler/backoff_stress.rs)

```rust
#[test]
fn stress_backoff_rapid_inject_park_cycle() {
    init_test_logging();
    
    let scheduler = Arc::new(Scheduler::new(4)); // 4 workers
    let injected = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
    let executed = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
    
    // Injector thread: rapid task injection
    let sched = Arc::clone(&scheduler);
    let inj = Arc::clone(&injected);
    let injector = thread::spawn(move || {
        for _ in 0..100_000 {
            sched.inject(dummy_task());
            inj.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
        }
    });
    
    // Worker threads: process tasks
    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..4).map(|id| {
        let sched = Arc::clone(&scheduler);
        let exec = Arc::clone(&executed);
        thread::spawn(move || {
            let mut worker = sched.worker(id);
            while !sched.shutdown() {
                if let Some(task) = worker.next_task() {
                    exec.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
                    worker.complete(task);
                }
            }
        })
    }).collect();
    
    injector.join().unwrap();
    scheduler.shutdown();
    for h in handles { h.join().unwrap(); }
    
    // CRITICAL: All injected tasks must be executed
    assert_eq!(injected.load(Ordering::SeqCst), executed.load(Ordering::SeqCst));
}

#[test]
fn stress_backoff_latency_distribution() {
    let scheduler = Scheduler::new(4);
    let latencies = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::new()));
    
    for _ in 0..10_000 {
        let start = Instant::now();
        let lat = Arc::clone(&latencies);
        
        scheduler.inject_with_callback(move || {
            lat.lock().unwrap().push(start.elapsed());
        });
    }
    
    scheduler.wait_idle();
    
    let lat = latencies.lock().unwrap();
    let p50 = percentile(&lat, 50);
    let p99 = percentile(&lat, 99);
    let p999 = percentile(&lat, 99.9);
    
    println!("p50={:?}, p99={:?}, p99.9={:?}", p50, p99, p999);
    
    // Verify no extreme outliers (lost wakeup would cause >100ms)
    assert!(p999 < Duration::from_millis(10), "p99.9 latency too high: {:?}", p999);
}
```

### Loom Concurrency Tests (tests/scheduler/backoff_loom.rs)

```rust
use loom::sync::{Arc, atomic::{AtomicBool, AtomicUsize, Ordering}};
use loom::thread;

#[test]
fn loom_backoff_inject_during_park() {
    loom::model(|| {
        let queue = Arc::new(Queue::new());
        let parker = Arc::new(Parker::new());
        let found_work = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));
        
        // Worker thread: backoff and park
        let q1 = Arc::clone(&queue);
        let p1 = Arc::clone(&parker);
        let f1 = Arc::clone(&found_work);
        let worker = thread::spawn(move || {
            // Simulate backoff loop
            loop {
                if !q1.is_empty() {
                    f1.store(true, Ordering::Release);
                    break;
                }
                // Simulate reaching park point
                p1.park();
                // After wake: MUST re-check
                if !q1.is_empty() {
                    f1.store(true, Ordering::Release);
                    break;
                }
            }
        });
        
        // Injector thread: add work and unpark
        let q2 = Arc::clone(&queue);
        let p2 = Arc::clone(&parker);
        let injector = thread::spawn(move || {
            q2.push(1);
            p2.unpark();
        });
        
        worker.join().unwrap();
        injector.join().unwrap();
        
        // Work MUST be found
        assert!(found_work.load(Ordering::Acquire));
    });
}

#[test]
fn loom_backoff_spurious_wakeup() {
    loom::model(|| {
        let queue = Arc::new(Queue::new());
        let parker = Arc::new(Parker::new());
        let iterations = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
        
        // Worker: may wake spuriously, should continue checking
        let q = Arc::clone(&queue);
        let p = Arc::clone(&parker);
        let i = Arc::clone(&iterations);
        let worker = thread::spawn(move || {
            loop {
                i.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
                if !q.is_empty() {
                    break;
                }
                p.park();
                // Continue loop, don't break immediately
            }
        });
        
        // Inject after spurious wakes are possible
        thread::spawn(move || {
            queue.push(42);
            parker.unpark();
        });
        
        worker.join().unwrap();
        
        // Multiple iterations means spurious wakes handled
        // Work was eventually found
    });
}
```

### Integration Tests (tests/scheduler/backoff_integration.rs)

```rust
#[test]
fn integration_backoff_with_io_driver() {
    let rt = Runtime::builder()
        .workers(4)
        .enable_io()
        .build()
        .unwrap();
    
    rt.block_on(async {
        // Mix of CPU and IO tasks
        let mut handles = vec![];
        
        for i in 0..1000 {
            if i % 10 == 0 {
                // IO task
                handles.push(spawn(async {
                    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_micros(100)).await;
                }));
            } else {
                // CPU task
                handles.push(spawn(async {
                    std::hint::black_box(fibonacci(20));
                }));
            }
        }
        
        for h in handles {
            h.await.unwrap();
        }
    });
    
    // All tasks complete - no lost wakeups
}
```

### Observability Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_backoff_metrics_emitted() {
    let scheduler = Scheduler::new_with_metrics(4);
    
    // Trigger backoff
    scheduler.inject(slow_task());
    std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100));
    
    let metrics = scheduler.metrics();
    assert!(metrics.spin_iterations > 0);
    assert!(metrics.yield_iterations > 0);
    assert!(metrics.park_count > 0);
    assert!(metrics.unpark_count > 0);
}
```

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Loop continues after park (no break)
- [ ] Both local and global queues checked after wake
- [ ] Backoff reset to spin phase after waking
- [ ] three_lane.rs checks both queues
- [ ] All 15+ unit tests passing
- [ ] Stress test: 100K tasks with zero lost
- [ ] Latency p99.9 < 10ms under load
- [ ] Loom tests: all interleavings verified
- [ ] Metrics: park/unpark counts tracked
